title: Sequence
subtitle: An ordered, homogeneous collection of bytes or numbers. Strings are Sequences with a character encoding.

members.description:
Multiplies the values of aSeq to the corresponding values of the receiver
    returning a new vector with the result.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric.

Raises the values of the receiver in the corresponding values of aSeq
    returning a new vector with the result.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric.

Raises the values of the receiver in the corresponding values of aSeq.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric. Returns self.

Multiplies the values of aSeq to the corresponding values of the receiver.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric. Returns self.

Vector addition - adds the values of aSeq to the corresponding values of the
    receiver returning a new vector with the result. Only works on Sequences
    whose item type is numeric.

Vector addition - adds the values of aSeq to those of the receiver.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric. Returns self.

Vector subtraction - Subtracts the values of aSeq from the corresponding
    values of the receiver returning a new vector with the result. Only works on
    Sequences whose item type is numeric.

Vector subtraction - subtracts the values of aSeq to those of the receiver.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric. Returns self.
Returns a copy of the receiver with aSequence appended to it.

Divides the values of the receiver by the corresponding values of aSeq
    returning a new vector with the result.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric.

Divides the values of aSeq to the corresponding values of the receiver.
    Only works on Sequences whose item type is numeric. Returns self.

members.name: Max
members.description: Returns the maximum value in the sequence.

members.name: Min
members.description: Returns the minimum value in the sequence.

members.name: abs
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to its absolute value.
    Returns self.

members.name: acos
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric arcsine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: afterSeq
members.description:
Returns the slice of the receiver (as a Symbol) after aSequence or
    nil if aSequence is not found. If aSequence is empty, the receiver
    (or a copy of the receiver if it is mutable) is returned.

members.name: alignCenter
members.description: Example: Io> "abc" alignCenter(10, "-") ==> ---abc---- Io> "abc" alignCenter(10, "-=") ==> -=-abc-=-=

members.name: alignLeft
members.description:
Example:

Io> "abc" alignLeft(10, "+") ==> abc+++++++ Io> "abc" alignLeft(10, "-=") ==> abc-=-=-=-

members.name: alignLeftInPlace
members.description: Same as align left but operation is performed on the receiver.

members.name: alignRight
members.description:
Example:

Io> "abc" alignRight(10, "-") ==> -------abc Io> "abc" alignRight(10, "-=") ==> -=-=-=-abc

members.name: append
members.description: Appends aNumber (cast to a byte) to the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: appendPathSeq
members.description:
Appends argument to the receiver such that there is one
    and only one path separator between the two. Returns self.

members.name: appendSeq
members.description:
Calls asString on the arguments and appends the string to the receiver.
    Returns self.

members.name: asBase64
members.description:
Returns an immutable, base64 encoded (according to RFC 1421) version of
    self. optionalCharactersPerLine describes the number of characters between
    line breaks and defaults to 0.

members.name: asBinaryNumber
members.description:
Returns a Number containing the first 8 bytes of the
    receiver without casting them to a double. Endian is same as machine.

members.name: asBinarySignedInteger
members.description:
Returns a Number with the bytes of the receiver interpreted as a binary
    signed integer. Endian is same as machine.

members.name: asBinaryUnsignedInteger
members.description:
Returns a Number with the bytes of the receiver interpreted as a binary
    unsigned integer. Endian is same as machine.

members.name: asCapitalized
members.description: Returns a copy of the receiver with the first charater made uppercase.

members.name: asFile
members.description: Returns a new File object with the receiver as its path.

members.name: asFixedSizeType
members.description:
Returns a new sequence with the receiver encoded in the
    minimal fixed width text encoding that its characters can fit
    into (either, ascii, utf8, utf16 or utf32).

members.name: asHex
members.description: Returns a hex string for the receiving sequence, e.g., \"abc\" asHex -> \"616263\".

members.name: asIoPath
members.description: Returns a Io style path for an OS style path.

members.name: asJson
members.description: Converts to form that could be interpreted as json if it already contains json, e.g. {"aaa":"bbb"} --> "{\"aaa\":\"bbb\"}"

members.name: asList
members.description:
Returns the receiver converted to a List containing all elements of the
    Sequence.

members.name: asLowercase
members.description: Returns a symbol containing the reveiver made lowercase.

members.name: asMessage
members.description: Returns the compiled message object for the string.

members.name: asMutable
members.description: Returns a mutable copy of the receiver.

members.name: asNumber
members.description:
Returns the receiver converted to a number.
    Initial whitespace is ignored.

members.name: asOSPath
members.description: Returns a OS style path for an Io style path.

members.name: asStruct
members.description:
For a sequence that contains the data for a raw memory data structure (as used in C), this method can be used to extract its members into an Object. The memberList argument specifies the layout of the datastructure. Its form is:

list(memberType1, memberName1, memberType2, memberName2, ...)

Member types include: 

    int8, int16, int32, int64
    uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64
    float32, float64
Example: 
    pointObject := structPointSeq asStruct(list("float32", "x", "float32", "y"))
The output pointObject would contain x and y slots with Number objects.

members.name: asSymbol
members.description: Returns a immutable Sequence (aka Symbol) version of the receiver.

members.name: asUCS2
members.description:
Returns a new copy of the receiver converted to UCS2 (fixed character width
    UTF16) encoding.

members.name: asUCS4
members.description:
Returns a new copy of the receiver converted to UCS4 (fixed character width
    UTF32) encoding.

members.name: asUTF8
members.description: Returns a new copy of the receiver converted to utf8 encoding.

members.name: asUppercase
members.description: Returns a symbol containing the reveiver made uppercase.

members.name: asin
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric arcsine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: at
members.description:
Returns a value at the index specified by aNumber.
    Returns nil if the index is out of bounds.

members.name: atInsertSeq
members.description:
Calls asString on object and inserts the string at position indexNumber.
    Returns self.

members.name: atPut
members.description:
Sets the value at the index specified by aNumberIndex to aNumber. Returns
    self.

members.name: atan
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric arctangent of its
    value. Returns self.

members.name: beforeSeq
members.description:
Returns the slice of the receiver (as a Symbol) before
    aSequence or self if aSequence is not found.

members.name: beginsWithSeq
members.description: Returns true if the receiver begins with aSequence, false otherwise.

members.name: betweenSeq
members.description:
Returns a new Sequence containing the bytes between the
    occurrence of aSequence and anotherSequence in the receiver.
    If aSequence is empty, this method is equivalent to
    beforeSeq(anotherSequence). If anotherSequence is nil, this method is
    equivalent to afterSeq(aSequence). nil is returned if no match is found.

members.name: bitAt
members.description: Returns a Number containing the bit at the bit index value.

members.name: bitCount
members.description: Returns the number of bits in the sequence.

members.name: bitwiseAnd
members.description:
Updates the receiver to be the result of a bitwiseAnd with aSequence.
    Returns self.

members.name: bitwiseNot
members.description:
Updates the receiver to be the result of a bitwiseNot with aSequence.
    Returns self.

members.name: bitwiseOr
members.description:
Updates the receiver to be the result of a bitwiseOr with aSequence. Returns
    self.

members.name: bitwiseXor
members.description:
Updates the receiver to be the result of a bitwiseXor with aSequence.
    Returns self.

members.name: byteAt
members.description: Returns a Number containing the byte at the byte index value.

members.name: capitalize
members.description: First charater of the receiver is made uppercase.

members.name: ceil
members.description:
Round each value to smallest integral value not less than x.
Returns self.

members.name: clear
members.description: Set all values in the sequence to 0. Returns self.

members.name: clipAfterSeq
members.description:
Removes the contents of the receiver after the end of
    the first occurrence of aSequence. Returns true if anything was
    removed, or false otherwise.

members.name: clipAfterStartOfSeq
members.description:
Removes the contents of the receiver after the beginning of
    the first occurrence of aSequence. Returns true if anything was
    removed, or false otherwise.

members.name: clipBeforeEndOfSeq
members.description:
Removes the contents of the receiver before the end of
    the first occurrence of aSequence. Returns true if anything was
    removed, or false otherwise.

members.name: clipBeforeSeq
members.description: Clips receiver before aSequence.

members.name: cloneAppendPath
members.description:
Appends argument to a copy the receiver such that there is one
    and only one path separator between the two and returns the result.

members.name: contains
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver contains an element equal in value to aNumber,
    false otherwise.

members.name: containsAnyCaseSeq
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver contains the aSequence
    regardless of casing, false otherwise.

members.name: containsSeq
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver contains the substring
    aSequence, false otherwise.

members.name: convertToItemType
members.description:
Converts the underlying machine type for the elements, expanding or
    contracting the size of the Sequence as needed. Valid names are uint8,
    uint16, uint32, uint64, int8, int16, int32, int64, float32, and float64.
    Note that 64 bit types are only available on platforms that support such
    types. Returns self.

members.name: copy
members.description:
Replaces the bytes of the receiver with a copy of those in aSequence.
    Returns self.

members.name: cos
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric cosine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: cosh
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the hyperbolic cosine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: distanceTo
members.description:
Returns a number with the square root of the sum of the square
    of the differences of the items between the sequences.

members.name: dotProduct
members.description: Returns a new Sequence containing the dot product of the receiver with aSeq.

members.name: duplicateIndexes
members.description:
Duplicates all indexes in the receiver.
    For example, list(1,2,3) duplicateIndexes == list(1,1,2,2,3,3). Returns
    self.

members.name: empty
members.description:
Sets all bytes in the receiver to 0x0 and sets
    its length to 0. Returns self.

members.name: encoding
members.description: Returns the encoding of the elements.

members.name: endsWithSeq
members.description: Returns true if the receiver ends with aSequence, false otherwise.

members.name: escape
members.description:
Escape characters in the receiver are replaced with escape codes.
    For example a string containing a single return character would contain the
    following 2 characters after being escaped: "\n". Returns self.

members.name: exclusiveSlice
members.description:
Returns a new string containing the subset of the
    receiver from the inclusiveStartIndex to the exclusiveEndIndex. The
    exclusiveEndIndex argument is optional. If not given, it is assumed to be
    one beyond the end of the string.

members.name: exp
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to e**value.
    Returns self.

members.name: fileName
members.description: Returns the last path component sans the path extension.

members.name: findNthSeq
members.description: Returns a number with the nth occurrence of aSequence.

members.name: findSeq
members.description:
Returns a number with the first occurrence of aSequence in
    the receiver after the startIndex. If no startIndex is specified,
    the search starts at index 0.
    nil is returned if no occurrences are found.

members.name: findSeqs
members.description:
Returns an object with two slots - an \"index\" slot which contains
    the first occurrence of any of the sequences in listOfSequences found
    in the receiver after the startIndex, and a \"match\" slot, which
    contains a reference to the matching sequence from listOfSequences.
    If no startIndex is specified, the search starts at index 0.
    nil is returned if no occurrences are found.

members.name: floor
members.description:
Round each value to largest integral value not greater than x.
    Returns self.

members.name: foreach
members.description:
For each element, set index to the index of the element and value to the element value and execute message. Example: 
    aSequence foreach(i, v, writeln("value at index ", i, " is ", v))
    aSequence foreach(v, writeln("value ", v))

members.name: fromBase
members.description:
Returns a number with a base 10 representation of the receiver
    converted from the specified base. Only base 2 through 32 are currently
    supported.

members.name: fromBase64
members.description:
Returns an immutable, base64 decoded (according to RFC 1421) version of
    self.

members.name: greaterThan
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is greater than aSeq, false otherwise.

members.name: greaterThanOrEqualTo
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver is greater than or equal to aSeq, false
    otherwise.

members.name: hash
members.description: Returns a Number containing a hash of the Sequence.

members.name: inclusiveSlice
members.description:
Returns a new string containing the subset of the
    receiver from the inclusiveStartIndex to the inclusiveEndIndex. The
    inclusiveEndIndex argument is optional. If not given, it is assumed to be
    the end of the string.

members.name: interpolate
members.description:
Returns immutable copy of self with interpolateInPlace(ctx) passed to the
    copy.

members.name: interpolateInPlace
members.description:
Replaces all #{expression} with expression evaluated in the optionalContext.
    If optionalContext not given, the current context is used.  Returns self.

members.name: isEmpty
members.description: Returns true if the size of the receiver is 0, false otherwise.

members.name: isEqualAnyCase
members.description:
Returns true if aSequence is equal to the receiver
    ignoring case differences, false otherwise.

members.name: isLowercase
members.description: Returns self if all the characters in the string are lower case.

members.name: isMutable
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is a mutable Sequence or false otherwise.

members.name: isSymbol
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver is a
    immutable Sequence (aka, a Symbol) or false otherwise.

members.name: isUppercase
members.description: Returns self if all the characters in the string are upper case.

members.name: isZero
members.description: Returns true if all elements are 0, false otherwise.

members.name: itemCopy
members.description: Returns a new sequence containing the items from the receiver.

members.name: itemSize
members.description: Returns number of bytes in each element.

members.name: itemType
members.description: Returns machine type of elements.

members.name: justSerialized
members.description: Writes the receiver's code into the stream.

members.name: lastPathComponent
members.description:
Returns a string containing the receiver clipped up
    to the last path separator.

members.name: lessThan
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is less than aSeq, false otherwise.

members.name: lessThanOrEqualTo
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is less than or equal to aSeq, false otherwise.

members.name: linePrint
members.description: Prints the Sequence and a newline character.

members.name: log
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the natural log of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: log10
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the base 10 log of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: logicalAnd
members.description:
Updates the receiver's values to be the result of a logical OR operations
    with the values of aSequence. Returns self.

members.name: logicalOr
members.description:
Updates the receiver's values to be the result of a logical OR operations
    with the values of aSequence. Returns self.

members.name: lowercase
members.description: Makes all the uppercase characters in the receiver lowercase. Returns self.

members.name: lstrip
members.description:
Strips the characters in aSequence stripped from the beginning of the receiver. Example: 

    "Keep the tail" lstrip(" eKp")
    ==> "the tail"

members.name: makeFirstCharacterLowercase
members.description:
Receiver must be mutable (see also asMutable). Returns receiver. 
  Io> "ABC" asMutable makeFirstCharacterLowercase
  ==> aBC

members.name: makeFirstCharacterUppercase
members.description:
Receiver must be mutable (see also asMutable). Returns receiver. 
  Io> "abc" asMutable makeFirstCharacterUppercase
  ==> Abc

members.name: max
members.description: Returns the maximum value of the Sequence.

members.name: mean
members.description: Returns the arithmetic mean of the sequence.

members.name: meanSquare
members.description:
Returns the arithmetic mean of the sequence's values after they have been
    squared.

members.name: min
members.description: Returns the minimum value of the Sequence.

members.name: negate
members.description:
Negates the values of the receiver.
Returns self.

members.name: normalize
members.description:
Divides each value of the Sequence by the max value of the sequence.
    Returns self.

members.name: occurrencesOfSeq
members.description: Returns count of aSeq in the receiver.

members.name: pack
members.description:
Returns a new Sequence with the values packed in.

    Codes:

    *: (one at the beginning of the format string) declare format string as
    BigEndian B: unsigned byte b: byte C: unsigned char c: char H: unsigned
    short h: short I: unsigned int i: int L: unsigned long l: long f: float F:
    double s: string

    A '*' at the begging of the format string indicates native types are to be
    treated as Big Endiand.

    A number preceding a code declares an array of that type.

    In the case of 's', the preceding number indicates the size of the string to
    be packed. If the string passed is shorter than size, 0 padding will be used
    to fill to size. If the string passed is longer than size, only size chars
    will be packed.

    The difference between b/B and c/C is in the values passed to pack. For b/B
    pack expects a number. For c/C pack expects a one-char-string (this is the
    same as '1s' or 's')

    Examples:

    s := Sequence pack("IC5s", 100, "a", "hello")
    s := Sequence pack("5c", "h", "e", "l", "l", "o")
    s := Sequence pack("I", 0x01020304)
    s := Sequence pack("*I", 0x01020304)

members.name: parseJson
members.description: Interprets the Sequence as JSON and returns a Map.

members.name: pathComponent
members.description: Returns a slice of the receiver before the last path separator as a symbol.

members.name: pathExtension
members.description: Returns a string containing the receiver clipped up to the last period.

members.name: percentDecoded
members.description: Returns percent decoded version of receiver.

members.name: percentEncoded
members.description: Returns percent encoded version of receiver.

members.name: preallocateToSize
members.description:
If needed, resize the memory alloced for the receivers
    byte array to be large enough to fit the number of bytes specified by
    aNumber. This is useful for pio_reallocating the memory so it doesn't
    keep getting allocated as the Sequence is appended to. This operation
    will not change the Sequence's length or contents. Returns self.

members.name: prependSeq
members.description: Prepends given objects asString in reverse order to the receiver.  Returns self.

members.name: print
members.description: Prints contents of a sequence.

members.name: product
members.description: Returns the product of all the sequence's values multipled together.

members.name: rangeFill
members.description:
Sets the values of the Sequence to their index values.
    Returns self.

members.name: removeAt
members.description:
Removes the item at the specified index and returns the value removed.
    Returns nil if the index is out of bounds.

members.name: removeEvenIndexes
members.description:
Removes even indexes in the receiver.
    For example, list(1,2,3) removeEvenIndexes == list(1, 3). Returns self.

members.name: removeLast
members.description: Removes the last element from the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: removeOddIndexes
members.description:
Removes odd indexes in the receiver.
    For example, list(1,2,3) removeOddIndexes == list(2). Returns self.

members.name: removePrefix
members.description: If the receiver begins with aSequence, it is removed. Returns self.

members.name: removeSeq
members.description: Removes occurrences of aSequence from the receiver.

members.name: removeSlice
members.description:
Removes the items from startIndex to endIndex.
    Returns self.

members.name: removeSuffix
members.description: If the receiver end with aSequence, it is removed. Returns self.

members.name: repeated
members.description: Returns a new sequence containing the receiver repeated n number of times.

members.name: replaceFirstSeq
members.description:
optionalStartIndex) Returns a new Sequence with the first occurrence of
    aSequence replaced with anotherSequence in the receiver. If
    optionalStartIndex is provided, the search for aSequence begins at that
    index. Returns self.

members.name: replaceMap
members.description: In the receiver, the keys of aMap replaced with its values. Returns self.

members.name: replaceSeq
members.description:
Returns a new Sequence with all occurrences of aSequence
    replaced with anotherSequence in the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: reverse
members.description: Reverses the ordering of all the items of the receiver. Returns copy of receiver.

members.name: reverseFindSeq
members.description:
Returns a number with the first occurrence of aSequence in
    the receiver before the startIndex. The startIndex argument is optional.
    By default reverseFind starts at the end of the string. Nil is
    returned if no occurrences are found.

members.name: reverseInPlace
members.description: Reverses the bytes in the receiver, in-place.

members.name: rstrip
members.description:
Strips the characters in aSequence stripped from the end of the receiver. Example: 
    "Cut the tail off" rstrip(" afilot")
    ==> "Cut the"

members.name: set
members.description:
Sets the values of the receiver to the sequences of numbers in the
    arguments. Unset values will remain unchanged. Returns self.

members.name: setEncoding
members.description:
Sets the encoding flag of the receiver (only the encoding flag,
    itemSize and itemType will change, no conversion is done between UTF
    encodings - you can use convertToUTF8, etc methods for conversions).
    Valid encodings are number, utf8, utf16, and utf32. Returns self.

members.name: setItemType
members.description:
Sets the underlying machine type for the elements.
    Valid names are uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64, int8, int16, int32,
    int64, float32, and float64. Note that 64 bit types are only available
    on platforms that support such types. Returns self.

members.name: setItemsToDouble
members.description:
Sets all items in the Sequence to the double floating point value of
    aNumber.

members.name: setItemsToLong
members.description: Sets all items in the Sequence to the long integer value of aNumber.

members.name: setSize
members.description: Sets the length in bytes of the receiver to aNumber. Return self.

members.name: sin
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric sine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: sinh
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the hyperbolic sine of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: size
members.description:
Returns the length in number of items (which may or may not be the number of bytes, depending on the item type) of the receiver. For example: 

 "abc" size == 3

members.name: sizeInBytes
members.description: Returns the length in bytes of the receiver.

members.name: slice
members.description: Deprecated method. Use exSlice instead.

members.name: slicesBetween
members.description:
Returns a list of slices delimited by startSeq and endSeq. 
  Io> "" slicesBetween("<", ">")
  ==> list("a", "b", "/b", "/a")

members.name: sort
members.description: Sorts the characters/numbers in the array. Returns

members.name: split
members.description:
Returns a list containing the sub-sequences of the receiver divided by the given arguments. If no arguments are given the sequence is split on white space. Examples: 
 "a b c d" split == list("a", "b", "c", "d") "a*b*c*d"
    split("*") == list("a", "b", "c", "d") "a*b|c,d" split("*", "|", ",") ==
    list("a", "b", "c", "d") "a   b  c d" split == list("a", "", "", "", "b",
    "", "", "c", "", "d")

members.name: split Returns a list containing the sub-sequences of the receiver divided by the given arguments.
members.description: If no arguments are given the sequence is split on white space.

members.name: splitAt
members.description:
Returns a list containing the two parts of the receiver as split at the
    given index.

members.name: splitNoEmpties
members.description: Returns a list containing the non-empty sub-sequences of the receiver divided by the given arguments. If no arguments are given the sequence is split on white space. Examples: "a b c d" splitNoEmpties => list("a", "b", "c", "d") "a***b**c*d" splitNoEmpties("*") => list("a", "b", "c", "d") "a***b||c,d" splitNoEmpties("*", "|", ",") => list("a", "b", "c", "d")

members.name: sqrt
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the square root of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: square
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the square of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: strip
members.description:
Trims the whitespace (or optionalSequence) off both ends: 

    "   Trim this string   \r\n" strip
    ==> "Trim this string"

members.name: sum
members.description: Returns the sum of the Sequence.

members.name: tan
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the trigonometric tangent of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: tanh
members.description:
Sets each value of the Sequence to the hyperbolic tangent of its value.
    Returns self.

members.name: toBase
members.description:
Returns a Sequence containing the receiver (which is
    assumed to be a base 10 number) converted to the specified base.

members.name: translate
members.description:
In the receiver, the characters in fromChars are replaced with those in the
    same positions in toChars. Returns self.

members.name: unescape
members.description: Escape codes replaced with escape characters. Returns self.

members.name: unpack
members.description:
Unpacks self into a list using the format passed in. See Sequence pack.

    Returns a List.

    Examples:

    s := Sequence pack("IC5s", 100, "a", "hello")
    l := s unpack("IC5s")

    s := Sequence pack("5c", "h", "e", "l", "l", "o")
    l := s unpack("5c")

    s := Sequence pack("I", 0x01020304)
    l := s unpack("I")

    s := Sequence pack("*I", 0x01020304)
    l := s unpack("*I")

    l := "hello" unpack("5c")

members.name: uppercase
members.description: Makes all characters of the receiver uppercase.

members.name: urlDecoded
members.description: Returns url decoded version of receiver.

members.name: urlEncoded
members.description: Returns url encoded version of receiver.

members.name: whiteSpaceStrings
members.description:
Returns a List of strings. Each string contains a different
    whitespace character.

members.name: with
members.description:
Returns a new Sequence which is the concatenation of the arguments.
    The returned sequence will have the same mutability status as the receiver.

members.name: withStruct
members.description:
This method is useful for producing a Sequence containing a raw datastructure with the specified types and values. The memberList format is:

list(memberType1, memberName1, memberType2, memberName2, ...)

Member types include: 

    int8, int16, int32, int64
    uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64
    float32, float64
Example: 
    pointStructSeq := Sequence withStruct(list("float32", 1.2, "float32", 3.5))
The output pointStructSeq would contain 2 raw 32 bit floats.
